titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Douglas Clyde Macintosh Papers
titlestmt.author: Compiled by Jane Thomson and Martha Lund Smalley

controlnote.p: This encoded findingaid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

conventiondeclaration.abbr: finding aid prepared according to local divinity library descriptive practices.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday September 2, 2020 at 22:30

did.unittitle: Douglas Clyde Macintosh Papers
did.abstract: Letters, writings, and memorabilia document Macintosh's life and work. Douglas Clyde Macintosh was Professor of Theology at Yale from 1909 to 1942.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Douglas Clyde Macintosh Papers (RG 50), Special Collections, Yale Divinity School Library.

chronitemset.event: Professor of Bible and Systematic Theology at Brandon College, Manitoba
Assistant Professor of Systematic Theology, Yale Divinity School
Chaplain to Canadian forces in France; YMCA worker with American troops
Dwight Professor of Theology, Yale Divinity School
Chairman of the Religion Department, Yale University
Denied U.S. citizenship by Supreme Court, for refusal in advance to bear arms in defense of U.S.
Dwight Professor of Theology and Philosophy of Religion

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This collection provides a good overview of Macintosh's professional life, which was spent primarily at Yale Divinity School. The correspondence in Series I is not extensive, but includes letters from colleagues such as John Baillie, Josiah Royce, and Henry Nelson Wieman. Macintosh's thoughts on theological subjects are expressed in a few letters, but most are more routine exchanges related to his writings, students, and engagements.
The Writings of Series II include offprints and published pamphlets as well as typescript manuscripts. One of these manuscripts, "The Plain Man's Soliloquy," has not been published.
The Notes of Series III include extensive handwritten notes taken by Macintosh and notes taken by a student in Macintosh's Systematic Theology course in 1940.

Most significant among the biographical materials of Series IV is a large scrapbook that includes correspondence, clippings, and documents related to Macintosh's citizenship case. A native of Canada, Macintosh was denied United States citizenship by the Supreme Court because of his stance against bearing arms.

did.unittitle: Correspondence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including from George A. Coe, Josiah Royce, George B. Foster

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including from John Baillie, William Ernest Hocking

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including from James B. Pratt, to Wilbur Smith

did.unittitle: Writings
In published format
"Empirical Theology and Some of Its Misunderstanders" The Review of Religion

title.part: God in a World at War

did.unittitle: "The Hope of Immortality" Religion in Life
"Is Belief in the Historicity of Jesus Indispensable to Christian Faith?" The American Journal of Theology
"Is Theology Reducible to Mythology?" The Review of Religion
"The Meaning of God in Modern Religion" The Journal of Religion
"The Method of Religious Inquiry" Religion in Life
"The New Christianity and World-Conversion" The American Journal of Theology
"The Pragmatic Element in the Teaching of Paul" The American Journal of Theology
"The Reaction Against Metaphysics in Theology" (DCM dissertation)
"Theology and Metaphysics" Twentieth Century Philosophy
"Theology, Valuational or Existential?" The Review of Religion
"Troeltsch's Theory of Religious Knowledge" The American Journal of Theology
"What is the Christian Religion?" The Harvard Theological Review
Book review
"A Conversation About God" Christian Century (scrapbook of clippings)
Typescript manuscripts
"The Plain Man's Soliloquy"
"Charles Allen Dinsmore as Minister and Theologian"
Ordination prayer
"The Reasonableness of Christianity"
"The Problem of Religious Knowledge"
Notes
Notes by Macintosh
Small notebooks
Small notebooks
Large notebook - bibliography of works on theology
Loose leaf notes, including taken from books in Macintosh's library
Notes on Macintosh's Systematic Theology course
Taken by H. E. Finger, Jr.
Biographical Documentation
"Out of the Wilderness: A Philosophical Biography of Douglas Clyde Macintosh" by W. Preston Warren
Biographical material: obituary, photographs, article about Macintosh
Centennial Tribute compiled by Paul Douglas Macintosh Keane: tributes from former colleagues of Macintosh
Bibliography of Macintosh's works compiled by Raymond Morris
Miscellaneous collected material
Biographical tribute to Emily Powell Macintosh, first wife of Douglas Clyde Macintosh
Pamphlet about Douglas Clyde Macintosh Fellowship in Theology and the Philosophy of Religion established by Hope Conklin Macintosh
Annotated Bible
Presentation copy of The Nature of Religious Experience: Essays in Honor of Douglas Clyde Macintosh" , signed by the authors of the essays

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This volume was received from Alexander Mack Memorial Library, Special Collections, Bridgewater College, Bridgewater, Virginia in 2013, a gift in honor of Dr. William W. Thomas and Douglas Clyde Macintosh. The book was received by Bridgewater as an estate gift from Dr. Thomas, professor of Religion and Philosophy, James Madison University, 1971-2003.

did.unittitle: Scrapbook related to Macintosh's citizenship case
